Most nix commands have super short names, which makes them quick to type but annoying to learn. Major file system realted commands include:
	pwd: print working directory
	ls: list files and directories
	cd: change directory
	mkdir: make a new directory
	rm: remove files and directories (delete)
	cp: copy files (source>destination)
	mv: move files or dirs (basically re-name)
	nano: a wee text editor (very basic but always available)
	curl: Download files from web of ftp
	wget: download files from the web
	tar -zxvf: untar (unpackage) Tar archive files
	gunzip: Unzip files
	$PATH: the places (dirs to look for programs)


## AWS EC2 Instance

Connect to my instance with:

ssh -i ~/Downloads/bimm143_kenny.pem ubuntu@ec2-52-38-208-66.us-west-2.compute.amazonaws.com



blastp -query mm-first.fa -db zebrafish.1.protein.faa -out mm-first.x.zebrafish.txt


Secure Copy files between machines, in this case from our instance to our laptop

scp -i ~/Downloads/bimm143_kenny.pem ubuntu@ec2-52-38-208-66.us-west-2.compute.amazonaws.com:



## Class 17 instance
copy and paste new ssh link 

chmod 400 "bimm143_kenny.pem"

ssh -i "bimm143_kenny.pem" ubuntu@ec2-34-217-120-172.us-west-2.compute.amazonaws.com

export KEY=~/Downloads/bimm143_kenny.pem
export SERVER=ubuntu@ec2-34-217-120-172.us-west-2.compute.amazonaws.com

#Run the variables:
echo $KEY
echo $SERVER


## MUST RUN THESE TWO COMMANDS AGAIN IF CLOSE TERMINAL
export KEY=~/Downloads/bioinf_barry.pem
export SERVER=ubuntu@ec2-34-210-54-74.us-west-2.compute.amazonaws.com

# Connect to your instance
ssh -i $KEY $SERVER

# Transfer results.tsv back to your current directory
scp -r -i $KEY $SERVER:~/results.tsv .
